observations: Global comma-to-period ratio improved from 0.593 in v4 to 0.661 in v4b, which lands inside the 0.6-0.85 range, but it still sits below the 0.72 target; the lowest-texture stretches remain in the early interrogation and final approach blocks (v4b lines 9-23, 249-269).

Mean sentence length is 12.1 words and CV is 0.767, both inside the global rhythm range; the chapter now undulates better between short tactical beats and longer analytical sentences (v4b lines 25-31, 117, 173, 247).

Long-sentence percentage rose to 19.4%, above the 14% ceiling, but the excess is concentrated in Jaina's analytical passages and reads earned by voice rather than padding (v4b lines 25, 49, 93, 117, 173, 247).

One-sentence paragraph percentage remains high at 35.8%; most of that comes from permissible dialogue turns, but narration singletons at lines 101, 119, 145, 175, 235, and 261 are the beats that most stress the metric.

Scene-type punctuation stays closest to spec in the custody/interrogation block (approx. 0.642 comma:period across v4b lines 3-125); the road and village-approach blocks run leaner at about 0.57 and 0.545, which is why the texture scorer still flags those zones.

fixes.rule: default cadence / telegram run — collapse clipped route fragments into one earned tactical sentence
fixes.before: She tracked the route without hesitation. Lordaeron farms. Southbound roads. River forks. Distances a mounted patrol would care about even if a mage did not.
fixes.after: She tracked the route without hesitation—Lordaeron farms, southbound roads, river forks, distances a mounted patrol would care about even if a mage did not.

fixes.rule: emphasisRule / hedge removal — keep the beat short without two consecutive hedged sentences
fixes.before: The corner of Sylvanas's mouth moved once. It might have been a smile. It might have been the rejection of one.
fixes.after: The corner of Sylvanas's mouth moved once. Almost a smile. Then it was gone.

fixes.rule: semicolon_pct — join paired clauses instead of stacking two flat sentences
fixes.before: It should not have irritated her that the word did. It did anyway.
fixes.after: It should not have irritated her that the word did; it did anyway.

fixes.rule: texture_desert / connective tissue — replace three clipped travel beats with one bridged motion sentence
fixes.before: The pines thinned to birch. The birch gave way to open country. The last ward-stone fell behind them.
fixes.after: The pines thinned to birch, then gave way to open country, with the last ward-stone falling behind them.

fixes.rule: texture_desert — braid the evidence list into a cumulative sentence instead of five isolated beats
fixes.before: The road answered before she could. Cold chimneys. Untended fields. A trough green with scum and filmed over at the edges. A cart left loaded in one yard with sacks gone dark from dew, no lantern hung near it, no dog barking at six armed riders coming fast along the road.
fixes.after: The road answered before she could: cold chimneys, untended fields, a trough green with scum and filmed over at the edges, a cart left loaded in one yard with sacks gone dark from dew, no lantern hung near it, no dog barking at six armed riders coming fast along the road.

observations: Narration stays concrete and field-usable from the opening sensory anchor through the village approach; lines 5, 7, 117, 157, and 237 all work through teeth, stone, copper, soil, and flesh rather than abstraction.

Jaina's arcane register remains functional instead of report-like at lines 25, 31, 93, 151, 173, and 247; terms such as ward lattice, leyline drift, and contamination are always tied to immediate action or diagnosis.
No therapy-speak, corporate diction, or modern colloquial filler survived; the chapter avoids the style-guide's banned vocabulary list in both narration and dialogue.
A POV-distance filter at former line 15 ('clear sense of') softened the cell entrance beat; v4b line 15 now puts the judgment on the page directly.

Two abstract/meta phrases slipped toward document language in v4 ('shape of the decision' and 'words like if and likely'); both are now grounded in concrete positioning or simplification at v4b lines 129 and 177.

fixes.rule: POV distance — delete filter phrasing and render the perceived judgment directly
fixes.before: Jaina had the clear sense of being counted with the rest of the furnishings and, for the moment, judged containable.
fixes.after: Jaina was counted with the rest of the furnishings and, for the moment, judged containable.

fixes.rule: planning vocabulary firewall — replace abstract decision-language with physical staging
fixes.before: By the time she reached the horses, everyone else had already moved into the shape of the decision.
fixes.after: By the time she reached the horses, everyone else had already moved into position.

fixes.rule: document-voice / meta-language — ground Jaina's self-edit in speech behavior, not word-list commentary
fixes.before: Jaina cut herself off halfway through the explanation she had been about to offer with her hands and a patient tone and words like if and likely.
fixes.after: Jaina cut herself off halfway through the explanation she had been about to offer with her hands and a patient, simplifying tone.

observations: Figurative density remains controlled at roughly five notable comparisons across 4.3k words, or about 1.2 metaphors/similes per thousand words, which fits the 1-3 per 1000 rule.

The strongest surviving figures are concrete and readable: the pot-lid image at line 121, the second-heartbeat image at line 127, the chapel hush at line 195, the bad-instrument-wire image at line 237, and the drag-toward-truth framing at line 269.
Several v4 comparisons drifted away from Jaina's preferred domains into softer or more anthropomorphic imagery; those were the main metaphor violations in the pass.
The road-read block now uses Jaina-shaped technical language ('the sequence began to skip,' line 141) where v4 had a more generic lantern simile.
No forbidden emotional-defense, storm, drowning, shattered, or light/dark moral metaphors appear in the chapter.

fixes.rule: source-domain compliance — cut anthropomorphic landscape phrasing
fixes.before: Pine closed over them in dark ordered ranks, the trunks set back from the road as if the forest itself had agreed to military use.
fixes.after: Pine closed over them in dark ordered ranks, the trunks set back from the road with military neatness.

fixes.rule: POV-specific metaphor source — replace generic lantern simile with Jaina-coded sequence language
fixes.before: She counted distances under her breath—thirty yards, fifty, a hundred—while the stones stood along the road like lanterns running out of oil.
fixes.after: She counted distances under her breath—thirty yards, fifty, a hundred—while the sequence began to skip.

fixes.rule: anthropomorphic abstraction — remove land given intention
fixes.before: This was the thing itself, broad and patient and sunk into the land as if it had always expected to get this far.
fixes.after: This was the thing itself, broad and patient and sunk into the land, certain it would get this far.

fixes.rule: comparison overload — make the buck's damage concrete instead of comparative
fixes.before: The buck's eyes were filmed over milk-pale, but it came on with frantic speed, antlers crusted dark as if it had already been putting its head through things that should have stopped it.
fixes.after: The buck's eyes were filmed over milk-pale, but it came on with frantic speed, antlers crusted dark from battering through things that should have stopped it.

observations: Average paragraph length sits at 32.2 words / 2.3 sentences, below the 40-60 word narration workhorse; the shortfall is caused mostly by dialogue-turn formatting rather than bloated chopping inside long narration.
The chapter still uses many single-sentence paragraphs, but the majority are clean dialogue exchanges, which the guide explicitly permits in pressure scenes.
Longer narration paragraphs at lines 5, 85, 117, 139, and 231 remain varied and do not overstack descriptors or exceed the maximum-without-break cap.
The road-transition cadence is strongest where motion and read are causally linked (lines 117-147, 157-191); it is weakest where beats fall into isolated single lines without connective tissue.

Paragraph-shape variety is decent on the page—dense opening block, rapid interrogation turns, long approach paragraphs, clipped combat beats—but narration singletons still push the one-sentence metric above target.

fixes.rule: telegram prose / paragraph cadence — reduce stacked singleton travel beats
fixes.before: The pines thinned to birch. The birch gave way to open country. The last ward-stone fell behind them.
fixes.after: The pines thinned to birch, then gave way to open country, with the last ward-stone falling behind them.

fixes.rule: connective tissue — fold action and contact into one sentence to keep the read continuous
fixes.before: Jaina dismounted before the mare had fully stopped and pressed her palm into the road.
fixes.after: Jaina dismounted before the mare had fully stopped, pressing her palm into the road.

observations: Attribution stays cleanly inside spec: the chapter overwhelmingly uses said or no tag at all, with no creative-tag drift.
Action beats do double duty throughout the interrogation and road scenes, especially at lines 51, 73, 85, 103, 113, 149, 171, and 243.

Untagged dialogue runs remain legible because the chapter limits active speakers and alternates clearly; the sharpest untagged cluster is the note/escort exchange around lines 53-81, and speaker identity never muddies.
Action-beat placement favors before-speech and adjacent beats, which matches the guide's preference and keeps Sylvanas's authority physical rather than editorialized.

Dialogue-to-narration ratio is 18.7% dialogue to 81.3% narration, well below the procedural-chapter target; that is the one major dialogue-dimension miss, but it is structural and not fixable without adding beats.
This reads as Jaina from line 3 onward: the first pressure comes through ward-hum in her teeth, then through lattice, lines, corruption, and field-read rather than social description.
Her syntax matches the fingerprint: medium-to-long analytical sentences at lines 25, 31, 93, 147, 173, and 247 are built by logic rather than ramble.
Her emotional register stays controlled and clinical—fear arrives as locked shoulders, copper taste, shaking hands, and irritation at being reduced to 'useful' (lines 117-119, 151, 205, 247).
Power posture evolves correctly: she begins in explanatory over-translation (lines 25-63), then drops the soft version once Sylvanas proves she can keep up (lines 169-181, 241-247).
Speculative comfort and exposition filter both pass: wards, leylines, and necromancy are treated as professional problems, not wonders, and Jaina never explains baseline magic to herself.
Adverbs total 9, or roughly 0.52 per 250-word page, well under the max-2/page floor.
Passive-voice candidates sit at 3.25%, safely under the 8% cap; most are result-state constructions rather than inert sentence bodies.
Show/tell stays on target: the chapter prefers bodily, tactical, and sensory evidence over named emotion (lines 47, 117, 157, 205, 237, 269).
AI-default process verbs are absent in the prose body; the chapter does not drift into noticed/observed/processed/catalogued narration.
Comparison count improved to 4 'like' and 2 'as if' instances after revision; 'as if' is now under the anti-slop cap, while 'like' remains slightly high but not at density that overwhelms the page.
Opening gambit is specification-compliant: line 3 is a sensory anchor that establishes body-state and magical pressure before any exposition arrives.
Exposition integration passes: lines 25-31, 49, 93, 151, and 173 deliver information only because the current tactical situation demands it.
Power dynamics pass cleanly in the cell scene: Sylvanas gets the short declaratives and interruption rights early, while Jaina's clauses are longer and more qualified until status begins to equalize.
Speculative integration is fully domesticated; wards, leylines, necromancy, and plague contamination behave like infrastructure and pathology, not spectacle.

Scene shape is strong: sensory opening, midpoint turn at the barrier drop, second turn at the roadside read, escalation through corrupted animals, then a hard approach into Millhaven. Humor is correctly sparse, with the mare's 'patient contempt' at line 135 reading as the last safe dry beat before the tone hardens.

fingerprintChecks.vocabularyLevel: pass — lines 25, 31, 93, 151, 173, and 247 keep her arcane vocabulary functional, field-adapted, and free of lab-report or therapist drift.
fingerprintChecks.sentenceRhythm: pass — analytical passages use built, complex syntax (lines 25, 49, 93, 147, 173, 247), though the chapterwide participial rate remains below her ideal texture target.
fingerprintChecks.metaphorDensity: pass — about 1.2 figurative turns per thousand words, inside spec, with the weakest off-domain comparisons trimmed at lines 139, 141, 147, and 199.
fingerprintChecks.emotionalRegister: pass — emotion is named rarely and clinically, with most pressure rendered through physical cues at lines 7, 117-119, 151, 205, and 247.
fingerprintChecks.thoughtPatterns: pass — she reasons by observation, inference, correction, and test; lines 33, 63, 93, 173, 177, and 181 all show the hypothesis chain working in-scene.
fingerprintChecks.sensorySignature: pass — magical structure and system-patterns dominate her attention, with bodily sensation entering when magic or danger interrupts thought (lines 3-7, 117, 151, 157, 237).

ruleViolations: Former v4 line 15 inserted a filter layer ('clear sense of'); revised at v4b line 15 to keep Jaina's perception direct.

Former v4 lines 139-147 used softer, non-Jaina comparison language ('forest agreed,' 'lanterns running out of oil,' land 'expected'); revised at v4b lines 139-147 to better match her professional frame.

Chapter texture remains slightly under her ideal connective-tissue target: participial density is 6.8% against a 9.0% floor, with the driest remaining stretches in P4-P11, P26-P36, P48-P55, P75-P82, and P124-P134.

flaggedZones.instruction: Add comma-plus-participle bridges between the last warning summary and Sylvanas's entrance so the interrogation setup runs as one tightening sequence instead of a chain of isolated beats.
In the Antonidas/note exchange, add one or two ', Ving' connectors around Jaina's answer and Cyndia's exit so the call-and-response does not flatten into bare transcript.
Link the yes/accusation/answer sequence with one compound or participial sentence so Jaina's refusal lands as escalation, not stacked short hits.
Join the gear-inventory and order handoff with compound clauses (', and' / ', while') to keep the room in motion instead of dropping into isolated command fragments.
Between map proof and barrier drop, add participial phrasing to carry Sylvanas's reading, Jaina's correction, and the release of the anchor stone through one continuous tactical turn.
The south-of-border confirmation still wants more connective tissue; add ', Ving' phrases that tie the staff read, escort repositioning, and roadside kneel into a single investigative flow.
Around the 'It's in the ground' deduction, replace one or two clipped declaratives with compound-complex sentences so Jaina's analysis sounds less chopped while staying urgent.
After Sylvanas says 'The rest I can get,' add one participial bridge or clause-pair so Jaina's internal shift and remounting do not break into discrete note-card beats.
On the final Millhaven approach, enrich the narration with additional connective syntax—especially participial tails joining motion, sound, and spacing—to lift texture score without adding beats.

textureFindings.priorityInstruction: Participial density is still the biggest gap at 6.8% against a 12.0% target, so any further revision should add comma-plus-participle connective tissue in the flagged approach and interrogation zones before touching the already-on-target compound, em-dash, semicolon, or short-sentence metrics.
